intro: Enteropogon acicularis is a perennial grass native to Australia, commonly known as Needle Grass. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating various conditions such as wound healing and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Enteropogon acicularis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
